message:
feat(e2e): add comprehensive E2E tests for ntm git operations

Add test suite covering:
- Git status on clean and dirty repositories
- Detection of untracked, modified, and staged files
- Branch tracking and commit info extraction
- Git sync dry-run functionality
- Agent Mail integration status
- Result format validation

Tests use isolated temporary git repositories to avoid affecting
the main repo. Includes detailed logging with [E2E-GIT] prefixes.

Closes: bd-22wdu

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Establish quantitative metrics for measuring agent session effectiveness.

## Scope
- Define metrics: completion rate, retry count, time to completion, token efficiency

## Acceptance Criteria
- Core metrics defined and documented
- Metrics are objective and measurable
- Score normalization for comparison
- Weights configurable per use case

## Technical Notes
- Consider task complexity as normalizing factor
- Avoid metrics that incentivize gaming","notes":"Implemented comprehensive effectiveness metrics in internal/scoring/metrics.go:

**Core Metrics (7 total)**:
- Completion: Task completion rate (tasks_completed / tasks_assigned)
- Retries: First-attempt success rate (1 - retries/max_retries)  
- TimeEfficiency: Speed vs estimate (min(1, estimated/actual))
- TokenEfficiency: Token economy (min(1, baseline/actual))
- Quality: Composite of tests_passing + no_regressions
- ErrorRate: Error avoidance (1 - errors/total_ops)
- ContextUsage: Context window efficiency (1 - avg_usage)

**Weight Configurations**:
- DefaultWeights: Completion 30%, Quality 25%, TokenEfficiency 15%, TimeEfficiency 10%, Retries 10%, ErrorRate 5%, ContextUsage 5%
- SpeedFocusedWeights: TimeEfficiency 30%
- QualityFocusedWeights: Quality 35%, ErrorRate 10%
- EconomyFocusedWeights: TokenEfficiency 25%, ContextUsage 10%

**Features**:
- RawMetrics.ToEffectivenessScore() for converting measurements to normalized scores
- WeightsConfig for file-based configuration with task-type overrides
- LoadWeightsConfig() for loading from JSON
- EffectivenessScore.WithWeights() for recomputing with different weights
- Comprehensive edge case handling (zero values, capping at 1.0)

 {"id":"bd-22vun","title":"E2E Tests: ntm errors command with real tmux sessions","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end tests for the ntm errors command with real tmux sessions and error injection.

## Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_ntm_errors.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-ERRORS]"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_errors_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up test session: $TEST_SESSION"
    tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting ntm errors E2E tests"

# Setup: Create test session
log "Setup: Creating test session with multiple panes"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -v
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -h
sleep 0.5

# Inject errors into different panes
log "Injecting test errors into panes"

# Pane 0: Python error
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.0" 'echo "Traceback (most recent call last):" && echo "  File \"test.py\", line 1" && echo "FileNotFoundError: test.txt"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Pane 1: Go panic
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.1" 'echo "panic: runtime error: index out of range [5]" && echo "goroutine 1 [running]:"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Pane 2: Normal output
tmux send-keys -t "$TEST_SESSION:0.2" 'echo "INFO: Everything is fine"' Enter
sleep 0.3

# Test 1: Capture all errors
log "Test 1: Capture all errors"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="$TEST_SESSION" 2>&1 || echo '{"errors":[]}')
log "Result: $RESULT"

ERROR_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.total_errors // .errors | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "Error count: $ERROR_COUNT"

if [[ "$ERROR_COUNT" -ge "2" ]]; then
    pass "Found errors in session"
else
    fail "Expected at least 2 errors, got $ERROR_COUNT"
fi

# Test 2: Filter by pane
log "Test 2: Filter by specific pane"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="$TEST_SESSION" --panes=0 2>&1 || echo '{"errors":[]}')
log "Result for pane 0: $RESULT"

HAS_PYTHON_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.errors[] | select(.line | contains("Traceback"))' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
if [[ -n "$HAS_PYTHON_ERROR" ]]; then
    pass "Found Python error in pane 0"
else
    fail "Missing Python error in pane 0"
fi

# Test 3: Check error structure
log "Test 3: Verify error structure"
FIRST_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.errors[0]' 2>/dev/null || echo "{}")
log "First error structure: $FIRST_ERROR"

HAS_PANE=$(echo "$FIRST_ERROR" | jq 'has("pane")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
HAS_LINE=$(echo "$FIRST_ERROR" | jq 'has("line")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")

if [[ "$HAS_PANE" == "true" ]] && [[ "$HAS_LINE" == "true" ]]; then
    pass "Error structure is correct"
else
    fail "Error structure missing fields"
fi

# Test 4: Non-existent session
log "Test 4: Non-existent session error handling"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-errors="nonexistent_session_xyz" 2>&1 || echo '{"error":""}')
log "Result: $RESULT"

HAS_ERROR=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq 'has("error")' 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
if [[ "$HAS_ERROR" == "true" ]]; then
    pass "Proper error for non-existent session"
else
    fail "Missing error for non-existent session"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E ntm errors Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

[[ $FAILED -eq 0 ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Test error capture from multiple panes
- [ ] Test pane filtering (--panes flag)
- [ ] Test error structure (pane, timestamp, line fields)
- [ ] Test non-existent session error handling
- [ ] Test Python traceback detection
- [ ] Test Go panic detection
- [ ] Proper cleanup on exit
- [ ] Detailed logging with [E2E-ERRORS] prefix","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T18:38:04.402775323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T07:02:27.598566315Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T07:02:27.598472669Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22vun","depends_on_id":"bd-38x1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-28n0d","title":"Implement shell environment detection","description":"Implement shell environment detection.

## What

Detect shell type and common configuration that affects agent operation.

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "shell": {
    "type": "zsh",              // bash, zsh, fish, etc.
    "tmux_plugin_detected": true,  // May cause issues
    "oh_my_zsh_detected": true,    // Common zsh framework
    "config_path": "~/.zshrc"    // Where to look for aliases
  }
}
```

## Detection Methods

### Shell Type
- Read $SHELL environment variable
- Verify against running shell process

### tmux Plugin Detection
Check common plugin locations:
- ~/.oh-my-zsh/plugins/tmux
- ~/.zshrc contains 'plugins=(... tmux ...)'
- ~/.tmux.conf sourced plugins

### oh-my-zsh Detection
- Check for ~/.oh-my-zsh directory
- Check ZSH environment variable

## Why This Matters

tmux plugins can:
- Alias the tmux command
- Add automatic session attachment
- Modify key bindings

Agents need to know to use /usr/bin/tmux directly when plugins detected.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:40:42.601068157Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787668899Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T05:20:17.787582285Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-28n0d","depends_on_id":"bd-18gwh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-28o9m","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing reasoning modes","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for listing/browsing reasoning modes

## Command Structure
```
ntm ensemble modes [subcommand] [flags]

Subcommands:
  list    List modes (default)
  show    Show full details for a single mode
```

## List Command
```
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., Formal, Causal)
  --tier string       Filter by tier (core|advanced|experimental)
  --all               Alias for --tier all (show all tiers)
  --format string     Output format: table, json, yaml (default: table)
  --verbose           Show full descriptions + failure modes
```

### Default Behavior
- **Default list = core tier only** (curated).
- If advanced modes exist, print a hint: `Use --all to show advanced modes`.

## Show Command
```
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id|code> [flags]
  --format string     Output format: text, json, yaml (default: text)
```

## Behavior
- `ntm ensemble modes` with no subcommand defaults to `list`.
- Accepts lookup by **mode id** (lowercase) and **mode code** (e.g., A1).
- JSON/YAML outputs include: id, code, tier, name, category, short_desc, outputs, best_for, failure_modes, differentiator, icon, color.

## File Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Dependencies
- Requires mode catalog loader (bd-qn9gc)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Default list shows only core tier
- [ ] Category + tier filters work
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON/YAML outputs include code + tier
- [ ] `modes` defaults to `list`","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:23.818764532Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069282507Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T01:53:42.069258051Z","close_reason":"Implemented as 'ntm modes' command (not 'ntm ensemble modes').  {"id":"bd-2hjn","title":"Task: Create ContextPredictor module","description":"## Purpose
Implement context window exhaustion prediction based on token velocity tracking.

## Module Location
internal/context/predictor.go

## ContextPredictor Struct
type ContextPredictor struct {
    samples      []TokenSample
    window       time.Duration    // Velocity averaging window
    pollInterval time.Duration
}

type TokenSample struct {
    Timestamp time.Time
    Tokens    int
}

type Prediction struct {
    CurrentUsage         float64
    TokenVelocity        float64  // tokens per minute
    MinutesToExhaustion  float64
    ShouldWarn           bool
    ShouldCompact        bool
}

## Key Methods
func (p *ContextPredictor) AddSample(tokens int)
func (p *ContextPredictor) PredictExhaustion(modelLimit int) *Prediction
func (p *ContextPredictor) calculateVelocity() float64

## Prediction Logic
velocity = (latest_tokens - oldest_tokens_in_window) / window_duration
remaining = model_limit - current_tokens

 {"id":"bd-2i96d","title":"Feature: Mode Contribution Scoring","description":"## Summary
Track and display how much each mode contributed to the final synthesis, enabling users to understand which reasoning approaches were most valuable.

## Rationale
When synthesis completes, users can't tell which modes drove the conclusions. Contribution scoring:
- Builds trust by showing reasoning transparency
- Helps users pick better mode combinations over time
- Identifies modes that consistently add/don't add value

## Implementation
1. Compute **ContributionScore** in synthesis (do **not** mutate ModeOutput):
   - Use deduped findings, final citations, and unique insights.
   - Unique insights determined via normalized finding text + evidence pointer + RedundancyScore normalization.
2. Store results in a `ContributionReport` attached to SynthesisResult / run metadata.
3. Display per-mode contribution in synthesis output and `ntm ensemble status --show-contributions`.
4. Optional: persist historical contribution summaries in ensemble state store for preset tuning.

## Scoring (Deterministic)
- Normalize counts into 0-1 ranges.
- Score = w1*unique_findings + w2*citations + w3*diversity_bonus.
- Stable ordering by score desc, then mode_id.

## Logging
- Log per-mode counts (findings, unique, citations) and final score.
- Log scoring weights and normalization ranges per run.

## Tests
- Unit: scoring math, deterministic ordering, and tie-break rules.
- Unit: unique-insight detection with overlap cases.
- Integration: multi-mode fixture with known overlaps.
- E2E: report/CLI/robot JSON include contribution data.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Each mode gets a contribution score 0-100
- [ ] Scores reflect actual impact on synthesis
- [ ] Dashboard shows contribution breakdown
- [ ] Historical data enables mode selection optimization
- [ ] Robot API includes contribution data in output
- [ ] Unit + E2E tests cover scoring and output schema

## Dependencies
- Requires ModeOutput schema (bd-1wnxl)
- Requires Synthesis System (bd-2qwm8)
- Requires RedundancyScore normalization (bd-254do)
- Requires ensemble state persistence for historical data (bd-25wul)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:05:10.928811795Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T07:02:46.787509907Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T07:02:46.787295433Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","depends_on_id":"bd-254do","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","depends_on_id":"bd-25wul","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","depends_on_id":"bd-2qwm8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","depends_on_id":"bd-3n3ti","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:27Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":19,"issue_id":"bd-2i96d","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Implementation Note

 {"id":"bd-35bn4","title":"Feature: Auto-Ensemble Selection - Recommend best preset from question analysis","description":"# Feature: Auto-Ensemble Selection

## Problem Statement
Users face choice paralysis with 9 ensemble presets. They ask:
- Which ensemble is best for my question?
- What's the difference between bug-hunt and root-cause?
- Am I picking the right one?

## Solution
Analyze question text and recommend the best-fit ensemble with explanation (deterministic, rule-based).

## User Experience
```bash
ntm ensemble suggest "What security vulnerabilities exist in the auth system?"
# Output:
# Recommended: safety-risk
# Confidence: HIGH
#
# Reason: matched keywords: security, vulnerabilities → safety-risk
#
# Alternatives:
#   bug-hunt (0.65) - If looking for specific implementation bugs
#   root-cause (0.45) - If investigating known security incident
#
# To run: ntm ensemble spawn safety-risk --question "..."
```

## Piping Support
```bash
# Get just the ID for piping
ntm ensemble suggest --question "What security..." --id-only
# Output: safety-risk

# Pipe to spawn
ntm ensemble spawn $(ntm ensemble suggest --question "..." --id-only) --question "..."
```

## Matching Algorithm (Deterministic)
Pattern-based (no ML) for reliability:

```go
var ensembleKeywords = map[string][]string{
    "project-diagnosis":   {"architecture", "structure", "codebase", "overview", "health"},
    "idea-forge":          {"feature", "improve", "add", "enhance", "brainstorm", "ideas"},
    "spec-critique":       {"spec", "requirements", "api", "interface", "contract"},
    "safety-risk":         {"security", "vulnerability", "threat", "compliance", "risk"},
    "architecture-review": {"design", "pattern", "refactor", "structure", "scalable"},
    "tech-debt-triage":    {"debt", "legacy", "cleanup", "deprecat", "upgrade"},
    "bug-hunt":            {"bug", "error", "fail", "broken", "issue"},
    "root-cause":          {"why", "cause", "investigate", "debug", "trace"},
    "strategic-planning":  {"roadmap", "priority", "plan", "strategy", "quarter"},
}
```

Scoring rules:
- Lowercase + simple stemming (suffix trim)
- Keyword count × weights
- Tie-breaker: preset priority order
- Confidence = normalized score → HIGH/MED/LOW

## Robot API
```bash
ntm --robot-ensemble-suggest --question="..."
# Returns: {ensemble_id, confidence, reason, alternatives: [...]}
```

## Logging
- Log matched keywords + scores
- Log chosen preset + confidence

## Tests
- Unit: scoring + tie-breakers
- Unit: id-only output
- E2E: suggest + spawn piping
- E2E: robot JSON schema + deterministic ordering

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] suggest command analyzes question text
- [ ] Returns top match with confidence score
- [ ] Shows 2-3 alternatives with scores
- [ ] Explains why each was matched/ranked
- [ ] --id-only flag for piping
- [ ] Robot API returns structured JSON","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:02:50.039679175Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T02:23:19.144079127Z","closed_at":"2026-01-26T02:23:19.143356436Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-35bn4","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35bn4","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-35bn4","depends_on_id":"bd-1kthb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:28Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-35chh","title":"Test: Unit tests for BeadScanner","description":"## Purpose
Comprehensive unit tests for AllocationCalculator tier calculations, ensuring correct agent allocation based on bead counts.

## Test Structure

```go
// internal/swarm/allocation_test.go
package swarm

import (
    "testing"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    "github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func TestCalculateTier(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name      string
        beadCount int
        wantTier  int
    }{
        // Tier 1: 400+ beads
        {"tier1_minimum", 400, 1},
        {"tier1_high", 1000, 1},
        {"tier1_exact_boundary", 400, 1},
        
        // Tier 2: 100-399 beads
        {"tier2_minimum", 100, 2},
        {"tier2_maximum", 399, 2},
        {"tier2_middle", 250, 2},
        
        // Tier 3: <100 beads
        {"tier3_maximum", 99, 3},
        {"tier3_minimum", 1, 3},
        {"tier3_zero", 0, 3},
        {"tier3_negative", -1, 3}, // Edge case
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Testing bead count %d, expecting tier %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier)
            got := calc.CalculateTier(tt.beadCount)
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTier, got, 
                "beadCount=%d should be tier %d, got %d", tt.beadCount, tt.wantTier, got)
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateProjectAllocation(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := &config.SwarmConfig{
        Tier1Threshold: 400,
        Tier2Threshold: 100,
        Tier1Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 3, Cod: 2, Gmi: 1},
        Tier2Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 2, Cod: 1, Gmi: 1},
        Tier3Alloc:     config.Allocation{CC: 1, Cod: 1, Gmi: 0},
    }
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    tests := []struct {
        name       string
        project    ProjectBeadCount
        wantCC     int
        wantCod    int
        wantGmi    int
        wantTotal  int
    }{
        {
            name:      "tier1_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
            wantCC:    3, wantCod: 2, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 6,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier2_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
            wantCC:    2, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 1, wantTotal: 4,
        },
        {
            name:      "tier3_project",
            project:   ProjectBeadCount{Path: "/proj/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
            wantCC:    1, wantCod: 1, wantGmi: 0, wantTotal: 2,
        },
    }
    
    for _, tt := range tests {
        t.Run(tt.name, func(t *testing.T) {
            t.Logf("Project: %s, Beads: %d", tt.project.Name, tt.project.OpenBeads)
            
            alloc := calc.CalculateProjectAllocation(tt.project)
            
            t.Logf("Result: CC=%d, Cod=%d, Gmi=%d, Total=%d",
                alloc.CCAgents, alloc.CodAgents, alloc.GmiAgents, alloc.TotalAgents)
            
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantCC, alloc.CCAgents, "CC agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantCod, alloc.CodAgents, "Cod agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantGmi, alloc.GmiAgents, "Gmi agents mismatch")
            assert.Equal(t, tt.wantTotal, alloc.TotalAgents, "Total agents mismatch")
        })
    }
}

func TestCalculateAllocations_Sorting(t *testing.T) {
    cfg := defaultTestConfig()
    calc := NewAllocationCalculator(cfg)
    
    // Input in random order
    projects := []ProjectBeadCount{
        {Path: "/p/small", Name: "small", OpenBeads: 50},
        {Path: "/p/big", Name: "big", OpenBeads: 500},
        {Path: "/p/med", Name: "med", OpenBeads: 200},
    }
    
    t.Logf("Input order: %v", projectNames(projects))
```

+// GitInfo contains git repository information
+type GitInfo struct {
+	Branch          string   `json:"branch"`

+	Dirty           bool     `json:"dirty"`
+	Ahead           int      `json:"ahead"`
+	Behind          int      `json:"behind"`
+	StagedFiles     []string `json:"staged_files,omitempty"`

+// NewGitTestSuite creates a new git test suite with a temp git repo

+// Setup creates a temporary git repository for testing
+func (s *GitTestSuite) Setup() error {
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-GIT] Setting up test environment")
+
+	// Create temp directory
+	tempDir, err := os.MkdirTemp("", "ntm-git-e2e-")
+	if err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("create temp dir: %w", err)
+	}
+	s.tempDir = tempDir
+	s.repoPath = filepath.Join(tempDir, "test-repo")
+
+	s.cleanup = append(s.cleanup, func() {
+		os.RemoveAll(tempDir)
+	})
+
+	// Create and init git repo
+	if err := os.MkdirAll(s.repoPath, 0755); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("create repo dir: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	// Initialize git repo
+	cmd := exec.Command("git", "init")
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("git init: %w, output: %s", err, string(out))
+	}
+
+	// Configure git user for commits
+	cmd = exec.Command("git", "config", "user.email", "test@example.com")
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	cmd.Run()
+
+	cmd = exec.Command("git", "config", "user.name", "Test User")
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	cmd.Run()
+
+	// Create initial commit
+	readme := filepath.Join(s.repoPath, "README.md")
+	if err := os.WriteFile(readme, []byte("# Test Repository\n"), 0644); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("write readme: %w", err)
+	}
+
+	cmd = exec.Command("git", "add", "README.md")
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	cmd.Run()
+
+	cmd = exec.Command("git", "commit", "-m", "Initial commit")
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	if out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
+		return fmt.Errorf("initial commit: %w, output: %s", err, string(out))
+	}
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-GIT] Created test repo at %s", s.repoPath)
+	return nil
+}
+
+// RepoPath returns the test repository path
+func (s *GitTestSuite) RepoPath() string {

+}
+
+// Logger returns the test logger
+func (s *GitTestSuite) Logger() *TestLogger {
+	return s.logger
+}
+
+// Teardown cleans up resources
+func (s *GitTestSuite) Teardown() {
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-GIT] Running cleanup (%d items)", len(s.cleanup))
+
+	for i := len(s.cleanup) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {
+		s.cleanup[i]()
+	}
+
+	s.logger.Close()
+}

+// RunGitStatus executes ntm git status and returns the parsed result
+func (s *GitTestSuite) RunGitStatus(flags ...string) (*GitStatusResult, error) {
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-GIT] Running git status flags=%v", flags)
+
+	// Change to repo directory and run ntm git status
+	args := []string{"git", "status", "--json"}
+	args = append(args, flags...)
+
+	cmd := exec.Command("ntm", args...)
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-GIT] Output: %s", string(output))
+
+	var result GitStatusResult
+	if jsonErr := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); jsonErr != nil {
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("command failed: %w, output: %s", err, string(output))
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse failed: %w, output: %s", jsonErr, string(output))
+	}
+
+	s.logger.LogJSON("[E2E-GIT] Status Result", result)
+	return &result, nil
+}
+

+
+	args := []string{"git", "sync", "--json"}

+
+	cmd := exec.Command("ntm", args...)
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
+
+	s.logger.Log("[E2E-GIT] Output: %s", string(output))
+
+	var result GitSyncResult
+	if jsonErr := json.Unmarshal(output, &result); jsonErr != nil {
+		if err != nil {
+			return nil, fmt.Errorf("command failed: %w, output: %s", err, string(output))
+		}
+		return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse failed: %w, output: %s", jsonErr, string(output))
+	}
+
+	s.logger.LogJSON("[E2E-GIT] Sync Result", result)
+	return &result, nil
+}

+// CreateFile creates a file in the test repo

+	if err := os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(path), 0755); err != nil {
+		return err

+	return os.WriteFile(path, []byte(content), 0644)

+
+// ModifyFile modifies an existing file
+func (s *GitTestSuite) ModifyFile(name, content string) error {
+	path := filepath.Join(s.repoPath, name)
+	f, err := os.OpenFile(path, os.O_APPEND|os.O_WRONLY, 0644)
+	if err != nil {

+	}

+	_, err = f.WriteString(content)
+	return err
+}

+// GetCurrentBranch returns the current branch name
+func (s *GitTestSuite) GetCurrentBranch() string {
+	cmd := exec.Command("git", "rev-parse", "--abbrev-ref", "HEAD")
+	cmd.Dir = s.repoPath
+	out, err := cmd.Output()
+	if err != nil {
+		return ""
+	}
+	return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
+}

+// TestGitMultipleFiles tests status with multiple file types
+func TestGitMultipleFiles(t *testing.T) {
+	SkipIfShort(t)
+	SkipIfNoNTM(t)
+
+	suite := NewGitTestSuite(t, "git-multiple-files")
+	defer suite.Teardown()
+
+	if err := suite.Setup(); err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-GIT] Setup failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Create various file states
+	// 1. Untracked file
+	suite.CreateFile("untracked.txt", "untracked content\n")
+
+	// 2. Modified tracked file
+	suite.ModifyFile("README.md", "\nmodified content\n")
+
+	// 3. Staged new file
+	suite.CreateFile("staged.txt", "staged content\n")
+	suite.StageFile("staged.txt")
+
+	// 4. Another untracked file
+	suite.CreateFile("another_untracked.go", "package main\n")
+
+	// Wait for filesystem
+	time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
+
+	result, err := suite.RunGitStatus()
+	if err != nil {
+		t.Fatalf("[E2E-GIT] Git status failed: %v", err)
+	}
+
+	// Should be dirty
+	if !result.Git.Dirty {
+		t.Error("[E2E-GIT] Repo with changes should be dirty")
+	}
+
+	// Count files
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-GIT] Multiple files test:")
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-GIT]   Staged: %d files: %v", len(result.Git.StagedFiles), result.Git.StagedFiles)
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-GIT]   Modified: %d files: %v", len(result.Git.ModifiedFiles), result.Git.ModifiedFiles)
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-GIT]   Untracked: %d files: %v", len(result.Git.UntrackedFiles), result.Git.UntrackedFiles)
+
+	// Verify we have files in each category
+	if len(result.Git.StagedFiles) < 1 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-GIT] Expected at least 1 staged file, got %d", len(result.Git.StagedFiles))
+	}
+
+	if len(result.Git.ModifiedFiles) < 1 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-GIT] Expected at least 1 modified file, got %d", len(result.Git.ModifiedFiles))
+	}
+
+	if len(result.Git.UntrackedFiles) < 1 {
+		t.Errorf("[E2E-GIT] Expected at least 1 untracked file, got %d", len(result.Git.UntrackedFiles))
+	}
+
+	suite.Logger().Log("[E2E-GIT] Multiple files test passed")
+}
